meta.title: EU Pay Transparency Directive — Member State Tracker
meta.subtitle: Where the 27 Member States Stand
meta.disclaimer: Status and sources verified on 28 April 2026. This page does not constitute legal advice.

green.label: Transposed or on-track
green.definition: National law adopted, or final bill confirmed to enter into force on or before 7 June 2026.

amber.label: Draft published
amber.definition: Bill or decree in formal consultation or before parliament; intent clear, deadline at risk.

red.label: No public draft / resisting
red.definition: No formal text published, only initial steps announced — or government has signalled non-compliance.

directivePillars: Pay range disclosed in job ads / before interview
Right of employees to information on pay & criteria
Ban on salary-history questions of candidates
Joint pay assessment if unjustified gap > 5%
Reversed burden of proof in equal-pay litigation

directiveSources.title: European Commission confirms 7 June 2026 deadline holds

SK.name: Slovakia
SK.headline: Equal Pay Act adopted 15 April 2026
SK.summary: First Member State across the line — law enters into force on the deadline (7 June 2026). Mirrors the Directive closely with national reporting templates.

sources.title: Slovakia becomes the first EU Member State to transpose

FI.name: Finland
FI.headline: Bill targeted to enter into force 18 May 2026
FI.summary: Government proposal submitted 22 December 2025; consultation closed 9 February 2026; final bill expected before the EU deadline. Tracks Directive thresholds (≥100 staff).

sources.title: Finland Targeting May 18 — Transposition of EU Pay Transparency Directive

IT.name: Italy
IT.headline: Draft legislative decree advancing through parliament
IT.summary: Council of Ministers gave preliminary approval on 5 February 2026; expected to meet the June 2026 deadline. The decree-driven model means rapid finalisation is possible.

keyDates.label: Preliminary approval by Council of Ministers

sources.title: Italy's Pay Transparency Decree — A Turning Point for Equal Pay

MT.name: Malta
MT.headline: Partial text already in force since 27 August 2025
MT.summary: Legal Notice 112 of 2025 brought pay-range disclosure and the salary-history ban into force early. Full alignment with the Directive expected by the deadline.

sources.title: Implementing the EU Pay Transparency Directive in Malta — New Obligations from 27 August 2025

NL.name: Netherlands
NL.headline: Cleanest draft — entry into force slips to 1 January 2027
NL.summary: Draft bill published; transposition will miss the EU deadline but the text is broadly considered the cleanest implementation in the bloc.

sources.title: Netherlands Delays Implementation of the Gender Pay Transparency Directive
EU Pay Transparency — Dutch implementation delayed to 2027

DK.name: Denmark
DK.headline: Draft 26 February 2026; effect 1 January 2027
DK.summary: Draft bill published 26 February 2026; consultation ran until 27 March 2026; targeted entry into force 1 January 2027.

sources.title: Denmark's Draft Pay Transparency Law — What Employers Need to Know

CZ.name: Czech Republic
CZ.headline: Draft 16 March 2026; rules from 1 January 2027
CZ.summary: Draft introduced by the Ministry of Labour on 16 March 2026; entry into force targeted 1 January 2027; reporting obligations phased in from 2028.

keyDates.label: Draft presented by Ministry of Labour

sources.title: Analyzing Czechia's Draft Legislation to Transpose the EU Pay Transparency Directive

PL.name: Poland
PL.headline: Draft December 2025 — tighter than the Directive
PL.summary: Draft published 16 December 2025; introduces shorter procedural deadlines than the Directive (e.g. 30 days instead of 60) and goes beyond the minimum requirements.

sources.title: Poland moves ahead with pay transparency
Polish Employers Receive Gift of EU Pay Transparency Obligations

RO.name: Romania
RO.headline: Revised draft consulted to 8 April 2026
RO.summary: Revised draft published 30 March 2026; public consultation ran until 8 April 2026; covers both public and private sector.

sources.title: Romania revises transposing legislation — key changes for employers

LT.name: Lithuania
LT.headline: Draft to Tripartite Council May 2025
LT.summary: Draft submitted to the Tripartite Council on 27 May 2025; obliges employers to take action where the gender pay gap exceeds 5%.

sources.title: Lithuania Set to Amend Labor Code to Align with EU Pay Transparency Directive

LV.name: Latvia
LV.headline: Draft 26 March 2026 — on track
LV.summary: Draft published 26 March 2026; consultation ran until 9 April 2026. On track to meet the EU deadline, with notable deviations from the Directive.

sources.title: Latvia on track for timely transposition — but with notable deviations

CY.name: Cyprus
CY.headline: Detailed draft consulted Nov–Dec 2025
CY.summary: A detailed draft bill was issued for public consultation between November and December 2025.

sources.title: Cyprus Sets Its Course on Pay Transparency in New Draft Bill

BG.name: Bulgaria
BG.headline: Adoption scheduled 29 May 2026
BG.summary: Adoption planned in parliament for 29 May 2026, just ahead of the EU deadline.

sources.title: Bulgaria Pay Transparency Reporting Law Guide

BE.name: Belgium
BE.headline: Public-sector partial; private draft pending
BE.summary: The French Community adopted a decree for the public sector (12 September 2024 / in force 1 January 2025). The Flemish Government is preparing a similar draft. Private sector transposition still pending.

sources.title: Decree partially transposes EU Pay Transparency Directive (Flemish public sector)

IE.name: Ireland
IE.headline: Pre-employment draft; pay-gap law already in force
IE.summary: The existing Gender Pay Gap Information Act is in force. A new draft (General Scheme of the Equality (Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill, January 2025) focuses on pre-employment transparency obligations.

sources.title: What's new for employers in 2026
Ireland — Gender Pay Gap Reporting

ES.name: Spain
ES.headline: Draft Royal Decree consulting 24 April – 8 May 2026
ES.summary: Public consultation on the draft Royal Decree implementing the Directive: 24 April to 8 May 2026.

sources.title: Spain moves to strengthen pay transparency and implement EU Gender Pay Transparency Directive

SI.name: Slovenia
SI.headline: Draft published 7 November 2025 for consultation
SI.summary: Draft transposition law published on 7 November 2025 and opened for public consultation.

sources.title: Slovenia — country page

DE.name: Germany
DE.headline: Expert report November 2025; bill not yet tabled
DE.summary: Final report of the Expert Commission published 7 November 2025, proposing a 'bureaucracy-reduced' model. No formal government bill tabled yet — the deadline is unlikely to be met.

sources.title: Final Report of the Expert Commission — Proposals for implementing the Pay Transparency Directive in Germany

FR.name: France
FR.headline: Preliminary draft to social partners March 2026
FR.summary: Preliminary draft sent to social partners on 6 March 2026; bill expected before parliament in late May 2026; entry into force targeted 1 January 2027 with key transparency provisions phased to 1 January 2028.

sources.title: France has disclosed a draft bill to implement the Gender Pay Transparency Directive
France Pay Transparency Draft Law — Key differences from the EU Directive
France pay transparency draft law

AT.name: Austria
AT.headline: Ministry working on draft; no published bill
AT.summary: The Ministry of Social Affairs is working on a draft. No formal bill has been published yet.

sources.title: EU Pay Transparency Directive — What companies in Austria need to know

PT.name: Portugal
PT.headline: No formal activity announced
PT.summary: No official government activity on transposition has been announced.

sources.title: Country update — April 2026

LU.name: Luxembourg
LU.headline: No known transposition activity
LU.summary: No known transposition steps on record.

sources.title: Member State Transposition Monitor

HR.name: Croatia
HR.headline: No domestic rules drafted
HR.summary: No national draft on record. Implementation is expected via amendments to the Labour Act, anticipated for spring 2026.

sources.title: Unlocking Pay Transparency in Croatia — Getting Ahead of EU Directive Implementation

GR.name: Greece
GR.headline: Specialist committee preparing framework
GR.summary: A specialist committee is preparing the framework. No published draft on record.

sources.title: Greece's EU Pay Transparency Directive Draft Still in Progress

HU.name: Hungary
HU.headline: No public updates
HU.summary: No public updates and no draft on record.

sources.title: Pay Transparency Directive in Hungary

SE.name: Sweden
SE.headline: Resisting — no bill planned (26 March 2026)
SE.summary: On 26 March 2026, the Swedish government called the Directive 'too administratively burdensome'. No bill is planned; the government is seeking renegotiation at EU level.

sources.title: Sweden calls for a renegotiation of the Pay Transparency Directive
Sweden seeks renegotiation — Is the tide turning against Brussels?

EE.name: Estonia
EE.headline: Resisting — would accept fine (16 April 2026)
EE.summary: On 16 April 2026, Estonia stated it would prefer to accept an EU fine rather than implement the Directive. Position has reportedly softened slightly since — verify before publication.

sources.title: Estonia seeks delay to EU equal pay rules despite a wide gender pay gap
Estonia opts for fines over implementation
